response.justification: This candidate is a genuinely strong generalist software craftsman with impressive testing culture and some high-throughput systems experience, but he does not meet the role's hard minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming; his professional C/C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with his systems work done primarily in D. He also lacks sustained production cloud-architecture experience and a formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, which are central to this compute-platform lead position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Exceptional testing and engineering-practice credentials: TDD advocate, author of test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran dojos, katas, and multi-day courses
Long, consistent track record shipping products across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform on Kubernetes
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work, and C++ is not even listed in his skills section
Limited production cloud-architecture and compute-platform experience; cloud exposure is mostly AWS CDK product work and self-hosted Kubernetes deployments
No formal technical-leadership role leading or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; leadership evidence is workshops and community teaching rather than owning team strategy
